dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8212 IH: Tech Diplomacy Training Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Baird introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs

form.official-title: To require Foreign Service officers to complete certain science, technology, engineering, and mathematics training, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Tech Diplomacy Training Act.

section.header: Requirement to complete certain science, technology, engineering, and mathematics training
section.text: Section 708 of the Foreign Service Act of 1980 (22 U.S.C. 4028) is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

paragraph.text: The Secretary of State, acting through the Director of the George P. Shultz National Foreign Affairs Training Center, shall establish and conduct training on—

subparagraph.text: matters related to science, technology, engineering, and mathematics, including—

clause.text: technological advancements made in different geographic regions;
how such topics affect diplomacy; and
any other training with respect to such matters that such secretary determines critical to the Foreign Service;

subparagraph.text: how the United States may utilize emerging science, technology, engineering, and mathematics matters as a tool of diplomacy; and

how United States adversaries and national security threats, as identified in the National Security Strategy most recently published by the President on the date on which such training is conducted, utilize technology—

clause.text: to undermine United States diplomacy; and

paragraph.text: The Secretary of State, acting through the Director of the George P. Shultz National Foreign Affairs Training Center, shall establish a condensed training curriculum on the matters described in paragraph (1).

subparagraph.text: The training described under paragraph (1) shall be included in the A–100 course attended by each Foreign Service officer.
With respect to an officer serving in the Foreign Service on the date of the enactment of the Tech Diplomacy Training Act and who has completed the A–100 course—

clause.text: the training described under paragraph (1) shall be completed not later than 18 months after such date of enactment; or
the training described under paragraph (2) shall be completed not later than 270 days after such date of enactment.

paragraph.text: The training required by this subsection shall be in addition to and conducted separate from each other training required to be completed by a Foreign Service officer.
